rulings.Case Title: ALLAN M. SCHUMAN VS. FOSTER, WARREN EDWARD AKA WARREN E. FOSTER AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Furher Response To Inspection Demand; And For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 16, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff ALLAN SCHUMAN'S Motion for an Order to Compel Further Response to Inspection Demand; and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Sanctions awarded against Defendant and his counsel in the amount of $350. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP JORDAN et al VS. DAVID CLIFTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 16, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DAVID CLIFTON's Motion For Leave To File cross-Complaint. Defendant's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted under CCP § 473(a)(1). Defendant Clifton has provided evidence that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory under CCP § 426.30, as it arises from the same transaction or occurrence as the allegations in Plaintiffs' complaint. Furthermore, Plaintiff has provided evidence that the delay in filing the compulsory cross-complaint was not in bad faith as he was incarcerated when the original complaint was filed, and Prisoner Legal Services was limited in assisting Mr. Clifton in this matter. (See Declaration of Sullivan in Support of Defendant's Motion to File a Cross-Complaint, 2, Exh. B.) Any challenge to the sufficiency or merits of the cross-complaint should be addressed after it is filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK VS. RUFINO R RIMANDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 16, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Plaintiff has failed to meet its burden of establishing that there is no triable issue as to any material fact. Plaintiff's separate statement (UMF 9) states that the total amount due and owing is $4,229.97. The declaration of Plaintiff in Support of Summary Judgment (signed by Richard Kier) states that $10,623.93 is owed, exclusive of court costs and attorneys fees. (Kier Decl. 12.) The proposed order and judgment seek the principal sum of $14,853.90 and $952 in costs for a total judgment of $15,805.90. Further, the separate statement cites to portions of the Kier Declaration that do not support the undisputed material facts in the separate statement. In addition, the separate statement cites generally to Exhibit A (which is 12 pages) and Exhibit B (which is 52 pages long and purports to be account statements) without specifically citing the page or pages of the exhibit with the pertinent information. (See Rule of Court 3.1350(d) ("Citation to the evidence in support of each material fact must include reference to the exhibit, title, page and line numbers.")) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA, A NATIONAL BANKING VS. ROBERT CHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Withdraw Pleading And Dismiss Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 16, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN's Motion To Withdraw Pleading And Dismiss Case. Granted, in part. Defendant's request to withdraw his general denial filed on November 13, 2012 is granted. (CCP § 473.) Plaintiff does not oppose the withdrawal of this pleading. Defendant's request to be dismissed from the action is denied without prejudice. Defendant presents no legal authority to support dismissal on the asserted factual grounds. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendants Omnimagnet, Llc, A Delaware Limited Liability Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 16, 2013, Line 18, Cross-Defendants Omnimagnet, Llc and Milgalter's Motion To Transfer Venue. Cross-Defendants Omnimagnet, LLC and Milgalter's motion to transfer venue to Los Angeles County is denied. Cross-Complainant Goodman's request for judicial notice is granted. The Court in Los Angeles has already ruled - on essentially the same motion filed in this court - that venue was not proper in Los Angeles and was proper in San Francisco. The newly cited case of Battaglia Enterprises, Inc. v. Superior Court of San Diego Cnty., 215 Cal. App. 4th 309, 317 (2013), as modified (Apr. 29, 2013) is of no effect here because Cross-Defendants cannot establish that the venue selection clauses in the parties' Operating and Employment Agreements would not "disrupt statutory venue provisions." (Id.) There is no statutory basis for venue in Los Angeles. Goodman has once again produced evidence that venue is proper in San Francisco. Moving parties have not shown that the convenience of the witnesses justifies transfer. Finally, the filing of the compulsory cross-complaint against Omnimagnet, LLC and Migalter is not a basis to transfer venue to Los Angeles. The Cross-Complaint is to be tried in the same county (San Francisco) that has been established as the proper venue for the original complaint. (K.R.L. Partnership v. Superior Court (2004) 120 Cal. App. 4th 490, 506.) Goodman's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
